# Navattic Alternatives for Interactive Demos (2026)

The best Navattic alternatives in 2026 compared: Stepshots, Storylane, Arcade, and Supademo — lighter, cheaper ways to put an interactive product demo on your site.

Hauke Jung|July 06, 2026|

4 min read

## The Short Answer

The best Navattic alternatives in 2026 are Stepshots (screenshot-based, open-source CLI, flat €19/mo), Storylane (guided HTML tours for sales and marketing), Arcade (polished demos from screen recording), and Supademo (simplest builder). Navattic is excellent at high-fidelity HTML product tours at scale — the alternatives win when you want something lighter, cheaper, or more developer-friendly.

## Where Navattic Fits — and Where It Pinches [...] The free tier includes one hosted demo and unlimited local recording with the open-source CLI. Try Stepshots free.

results.title: Storylane Alternatives in 2026: The Buyer Guide for PMM ...
results.content:
Storylane alternatives in 2026 are evaluated by B2B SaaS PMM teams when Storylane's customization ceiling, AI depth, or single-format output stops fitting the team's demo workflow. Arcade is the strongest Storylane alternative because it pairs interactive demo creation with AI narration via Avery, optional HTML capture on Enterprise, and a multi-format output flow that produces video alongside the demo from the same source recording. Navattic, Supademo, Walnut, Reprise, and HowdyGo cover the remaining segments (deep HTML fidelity, low-cost simplicity, sales personalization), but no other tool matches Arcade's AI + visual + video combination. [...] Quick Answer: Storylane Alternatives

 Best overall Storylane alternative: Arcade. AI narration, multi-format output, free plan (1 demo + 1 video, watermarked), Growth at $42.50/seat/month.
 Best for deep HTML fidelity: Navattic. Highest enterprise HTML cloning.
 Best low-cost simple tool: Supademo or HowdyGo. $27 and $16 per user respectively.
 Best for enterprise sales personalization: Walnut. Per-prospect demo customization.
 Best for sandboxed product replication: Reprise. API-level cloning.
 When to NOT switch: If Storylane's HTML capture at $40/user is working and you do not need AI or video generation.

## Why Do Teams Look for Storylane Alternatives in 2026?

Teams search for Storylane competitors and Storylane replacement options when one of three patterns emerges in real usage: [...] If none of these resonate, Storylane is probably still the right tool. If one or more does, the alternatives below address each gap directly.

## How Do the Top Storylane Alternatives Compare in 2026?

The interactive demo category in 2026 has seven platforms most B2B SaaS PMM teams evaluate when looking for tools like Storylane: Arcade, Navattic, Supademo, Walnut, Reprise, HowdyGo, and Demostack. Side-by-side, the differences are structural.

# The Best Storylane Alternatives in 2026: Interactive Demo Software Compared
## Supademo
755 subscribers

### Description
87 views
Posted: 23 Jun 2026
Looking for a Storylane alternative? I tested the 9 best interactive demo tools of 2026 — Supademo, Navattic, Arcade, HowdyGo, Walnut, Reprise, Demostack, Consensus, and Vidyard — and grouped them by the job you're actually hiring them for, so you can pick the right one fast.

Storylane is a genuinely good sales-demo-automation tool, but a lot of teams hit the same walls: personalization is gated behind the $500/mo Growth plan, the free plan gives you just 1 demo, and the new RepX AI agent is a separate product starting at $2,000/mo. If any of that pushed you to look around, this is your shortlist. [...] FAQ 
Q: What is the best Storylane alternative?
A: It depends on the job. For one platform across sales, marketing, onboarding, and support, Supademo is the best all-rounder. For HTML marketing demos, Navattic or Arcade. For enterprise cloned environments, Reprise or Demostack. For video buyer enablement, Consensus.

Q: Is Supademo better than Storylane?
A: For teams that need affordable access to personalization, more demo formats, and built-in AI (voice cloning + AI audit), yes. Storylane is strongest for sales demo automation with deal intelligence and its RepX agent at higher tiers. [...] improve it. And also, the free plan gives you five demos rather than just one, so you can actually see if it's working for you. And even if you don't become a paying customer, those demos that you made become yours forever. Now, let me share one honest limitation. Now, we have an AI demo agent that qualifies leads before passing them to a rep, but we don't have an AI avatar the way Storylane does. And that's because we have some strong opinions on when an AI avatar is actually necessary, but we'll leave that for another video. But if these are must-haves for you, factor that in. All right, so category two is inbound marketing and HTML editing. We've got Navattic, Arcade, and HowdyGo. Navattic is the HTML first pick. Think high-fidelity tours from your live product. # 7 Storylane Alternatives & Competitors For Interactive Demos

By Ryan Prior

October 12, 2023

11 Min read

Storylane is a fast-growing, YC-backed interactive demo software. If you find yourself here, then I'll assume a) you're familiar with them, and b) that Storylane might not be a fit for some reason.

 Per-seat pricing is too expensive as your team grows
 Some features or integrations might be missing
 You want something fully dedicated to sales use cases

…Whatever the reason, here's 7 of their competitors that might make a suitable alternative.

## Here are the 7 best alternatives to Storylane

1. Navattic
2. Supademo
3. Walnut
4. Reprise
5. Tourial
6. Arcade
7. Lancey [...] Overall though, it's a clean & accessible product with all the core offerings that you may want to consider.

### Lancey vs Storylane

 Both tools bill per user seat and have relatively accessible pricing
 Lancey enables HTML capture on all plans, whereas this is $100+ in Storylane
 Storylane has more native integrations (e.g. Clearbit, Segment, Zapier)
 Storylane has more customizable lead forms

### Pricing

You can publish 1 demo for free.

Paid plans start from $29 per month per seat.

Learn More About Lancey

## Final thoughts on Storylane competitors

If you're looking at Storylane alternatives for a marketing use case, I'd consider looking at:

 Mature tools (comparable costs): Navattic, Tourial, Arcade
 Newer tools (lower costs): Supademo, Lancey

results.title: 7 Best Navattic Alternatives + Interactive quiz (2026)
results.content:
## FAQs

### What are the best alternatives to Navattic?

The top Navattic alternatives in 2026 are HowdyGo, Storylane, Walnut, Arcade, Consensus, and TestBox. HowdyGo is the best overall alternative for teams wanting HTML demos at a lower price point ($159/month vs Navattic's $500/month). Storylane suits teams looking for screenshot demos. Walnut and TestBox cater to enterprise sales engineering teams with complex technical requirements.

### What is Navattic's pricing and how does it compare to alternatives? [...] The best alternative depends on your specific needs:

For teams wanting HTML demos like Navattic but more affordable: HowdyGo ($159/month vs $500/month)

For rapid demo creation with AI aids: Supademo (30-second demo creation, AI features)

For multiple demo formats and content hub: Storylane (screenshot, video, HTML demos)

For enterprise sales requiring CRM analytics: Walnut (deep CRM integration, advanced tracking)

For design-centric product tours: Arcade (visually polished marketing content)

For video-first, branching demos: Consensus (automated video journeys)

For full sandbox demos in enterprise sales: TestBox (live product overlays, complex environments)

### Navattic alternatives and AI product tours [...] + 1. HowdyGo - Best Overall Alternative
  + 2. Supademo - Speed and Screenshots/Videos
  + 3. Storylane - Best for Multi-Format Demos
  + 4. Walnut - Suited for Enterprise Sales
  + 5. Arcade - Strong for Visual Storytelling
  + 6. Consensus – Video-Driven Demos for B2B Sales
  + 7. TestBox – Live Sandbox Environments (Not Walkthroughs)
